---
title: "GLEAM quick start: spatial Seurat workflow"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{GLEAM quick start: spatial Seurat workflow}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(GLEAM)
```

## Goal

This quick start demonstrates:

1. Spatial Seurat input
2. Pathway scoring with built-in Hallmark genesets
3. Region/sample-aware differential testing
4. Spatial score mapping with explicit slice/tissue context

## 1) Load/create spatial-capable Seurat object

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(Seurat)
data("spatial_medium_expr", package = "GLEAM")
data("spatial_medium_meta", package = "GLEAM")

sp_seu <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = spatial_medium_expr, meta.data = spatial_medium_meta)
DefaultAssay(sp_seu) <- "RNA"
sp_seu <- NormalizeData(sp_seu)
sp_seu <- FindVariableFeatures(sp_seu)
sp_seu <- ScaleData(sp_seu)
sp_seu <- RunPCA(sp_seu)
sp_seu <- RunUMAP(sp_seu, dims = 1:20)
```

## 2) Score pathways

```{r, eval=FALSE}
gs <- get_geneset("hallmark", source = "builtin", species = "human")
sp <- score_pathway(
  object = sp_seu,
  geneset = gs,
  geneset_source = "list",
  seurat = TRUE,
  assay = "RNA",
  layer = "data",
  slot = "data",
  method = "rank"
)
```

## 3) Spatial score plots (slice + coordinates)

```{r, eval=FALSE}
coords <- data.frame(
  x = sp_seu@meta.data$x,
  y = sp_seu@meta.data$y,
  row.names = rownames(sp_seu@meta.data)
)

# Simulated tissue-like background for slice-context demonstration
tissue_bg <- as.raster(matrix(colorRampPalette(c("#f7f3e8", "#eadfca", "#d9c7a4"))(256), nrow = 16))

plot_spatial_score(
  sp,
  pathway = rownames(sp$score)[1],
  coords = coords,
  image = tissue_bg,
  split.by = "sample"
)
```

## 4) Spatial differential testing

```{r, eval=FALSE}
sp_res <- test_pathway(
  score = sp,
  region = "region",
  group = "group",
  sample = "sample",
  level = "sample_region",
  method = "wilcox"
)

top_sp <- sp_res$table$pathway[order(sp_res$table$p_adj)][1]
plot_spatial_score(sp, pathway = top_sp, coords = coords, image = tissue_bg, split.by = "region")
plot_spatial_compare(sp_res)
```
